description: Cross-paper edges of the sphere-packing DAG. Direction reads subject-verb-object: '{from} {type} {to}'. The program has a clear structural arc: Cohn–Elkies (`math/0110009`) sets up the LP framework and conjectures magic functions for dimensions 2, 8, 24; Viazovska (`1603.04246`) realizes the conjecture for $E_8$, with CKMRV (`1603.06518`) extending to Leech; Cohn–Miller (`1603.04759`) studies the structure of the magic functions; Radchenko–Viazovska (`1701.00265`) abstracts the construction into Fourier interpolation; the early Cohn–Kumar framework (`math/0607446`) generalizes to universal optimality and conjectures it for $E_8$/Leech, which CKMRV (`1902.05438`) then proves. The Lean formalization (`2604.23468`) closes the loop by formally verifying Viazovska's theorem. No cross-DAG edges (sphere packing is a standalone island in the registry).

edges.note: Viazovska's restatement of the Cohn–Elkies LP bound is verbatim Theorem 2 of math/0110009 (the magic-function form).
CKMRV's restatement of the LP bound for the Leech proof is verbatim Theorem 2 of math/0110009.
Cohn–Miller's restated LP bound is Theorem 1 of math/0110009 (the general admissible-function form).
Viazovska's $E_8$ theorem proves the magic-function existence conjecture (Conjecture 2) of Cohn–Elkies for $n=8$.
Viazovska's theorem also confirms the refined root-structure conjecture for $n=8$: $g$ and $\widehat{g}$ vanish exactly at $E_8$ vector lengths.
CKMRV's Leech theorem proves the magic-function existence conjecture for $n=24$.
The Leech optimality theorem extends Viazovska's $E_8$ template to dimension 24 with different modular forms.
Cohn–Miller's Conjecture 2.2 is the Cohn–Elkies magic-function conjecture (Conjecture 2) restated with explicit $r$ values.
Cohn–Miller's polynomial-times-Gaussian numerical construction is exactly the polynomial method whose convergence Cohn–Elkies Conjecture 3 predicts.
Cohn–Miller's energy-minimization conjecture (6.1) is the energy reformulation of the magic-function existence conjecture.
Radchenko–Viazovska abstract Viazovska's $E_8$ magic-function existence into a free-standing Fourier-interpolation theorem on $\mathbb{R}$.
The Radchenko–Viazovska eigenfunction basis is the abstract form of the Leech $+1$-eigenfunction.
The Schwartz eigenfunction $b_m^\varepsilon$ in Radchenko–Viazovska is the systematic family containing Viazovska's specific $+1$-eigenfunction $a$.
The CKMRV universal-optimality theorem proves the Cohn–Kumar conjecture (32) for $E_8$ and Leech.
CKMRV's kernel-positivity reduction realizes the Euclidean universal-optimality criterion of Cohn–Kumar (Proposition 31).
The CKMRV higher-dimensional interpolation formula generalizes Radchenko–Viazovska's $\mathbb{R}^1$ Fourier interpolation, with derivative data and dimension-dependent sampling sets.
Universal optimality extends $E_8$ sphere-packing optimality to all completely monotonic pairwise energies.
Universal optimality extends Leech sphere-packing optimality.
The uniqueness corollary settles part of Cohn–Miller's Conjecture 2.2 by showing the optimal auxiliary functions are unique in dimensions 8 and 24.
CKMRV prove Cohn–Miller's energy-minimization conjecture (6.1) for $E_8$ and Leech, as the Gaussian-potential case of universal optimality.
The Lean formalization paper formalizes Viazovska's $E_8$ theorem verbatim — the informal statement and its formal Lean counterpart, captured as a direct cross-paper edge.
The Lean formalization restates the Cohn–Elkies LP bound (math/0110009 Theorem 2) as the framework Viazovska's formalized proof plugs into.
